description_long: This is a vintage, early 20th-century monoplane with a fabric-covered, straight wing and an open framework fuselage. The wings are mounted high on the fuselage and are supported by visible struts and wires, giving the aircraft a lightweight and skeletal appearance. The front of the airplane features a wooden propeller attached to a visible engine, with the cockpit situated just behind the engine and exposed to the elements. The tail section consists of a horizontal stabilizer and a single vertical fin, both also covered in fabric and supported by a lattice of struts. The landing gear is a simple, fixed taildragger configuration with two main wheels and a small tail skid. The overall color palette is dominated by tan and brown tones, with metallic and wooden structural elements visible throughout.
description: A vintage, fabric-covered monoplane with a high wing, open cockpit, and exposed framework, featuring a wooden propeller and simple taildragger landing gear.
description_view_0: Side view showing the high-mounted wing, open cockpit, exposed engine, and tail structure with lattice framework.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the full wingspan, symmetrical wing shape, and alignment of the tail surfaces.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the wing, cockpit, and tail, with clear visibility of the landing gear and structural struts.
description_view_3: Front view emphasizing the wooden propeller, engine, and the symmetrical arrangement of the wings and tail.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing monoplane with straight, unswept wings and external bracing struts; no winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles visible.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizer; fabric-covered surfaces and external bracing.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open cockpit located just behind the engine; single-seat configuration with no canopy.
